global.title: Secretary Hegseth grilled as Iran war costs reach $25 billion - Modern Ghana
global.summary: A U.S. congressional hearing focused on the mounting financial and strategic cost of the war with Iran, with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defending the operation as expenses rise sharply.
global.whyItMatters: This helps frame prayers and preaching around war, stewardship, fear, and the human cost of conflict.

global.title: King Charles III addresses a joint meeting of Congress - CBS News
global.summary: King Charles III’s address to Congress marked a notable moment in U.S.-U.K. relations during a tense international season, highlighting diplomacy and shared public symbolism.
global.whyItMatters: Pastors often interpret public moments of national symbolism and can use this to reflect on leadership, unity, and public witness.

global.title: Wall Street watches Fed and Big Tech as Iran tensions continue to unsettle markets - KITCO
global.summary: Markets were mixed ahead of major earnings and the Federal Reserve, while uncertainty around Iran’s latest proposal and shipping disruptions continued to weigh on investors.
global.whyItMatters: Economic anxiety shapes the emotional and financial reality of many congregants; this offers context for pastoral care and practical preaching.

canadian.title: Liberals pledge $6B investment in skilled trades workforce - CP24
canadian.summary: A new federal pledge would invest $6 billion in skilled trades training and workforce development, aimed at addressing labour shortages and expanding job pathways.
canadian.whyItMatters: Churches serving families, young adults, and job seekers may find this relevant for community support, mentoring, and vocational encouragement.

canadian.title: Canada to host headquarters of future NATO-linked financial institution - AP News
canadian.summary: Canada has been selected to host the headquarters of a new multilateral bank tied to defense financing, signaling a larger role in global security and military investment.
canadian.whyItMatters: This is a meaningful development in Canada’s public role on the world stage and may shape conversations about peace, defense, and national priorities.

canadian.title: Canada Playbook reports Vancouver hosting FIFA Congress and new defense-bank news - Politico
canadian.summary: Politico highlights fresh national developments, including the FIFA Congress in Vancouver and confirmation that Canada won the bid to host a new multilateral defense bank headquarters.
canadian.whyItMatters: It gives useful context for how Canada is being positioned internationally, including developments with direct relevance to British Columbia.

local.title: FIFA Congress gets underway at the Vancouver Convention Centre - Politico
local.summary: A major international soccer gathering is underway in Vancouver, bringing global attention and diplomatic traffic to the city as World Cup preparations continue.
local.whyItMatters: For a B.C. pastor, this is a timely local development that may affect civic life, hospitality, traffic, and opportunities for community engagement.

persecutedChurch.title: Vatican says anti-Christian violence has crossed a ‘red line’ in Israel - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: A Vatican representative urged Israeli authorities to act more decisively after repeated verbal and physical attacks on Christians, warning that the situation has become intolerable.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a direct call to prayer for vulnerable Christian communities and a reminder to teach the church about global solidarity.

persecutedChurch.title: Settlers reportedly attack Palestinian village in northern West Bank - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: Reports say Israeli settlers hurled stones and set property on fire in the West Bank village of Qusra, adding to instability and fear in the region.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Pastors should be attentive to violence affecting civilians in the Holy Land and lead congregations in prayer for peace and protection.

leadership.title: From teamwork to crew: what Artemis II reveals about character - Forbes
leadership.summary: A Forbes reflection on the Artemis II mission argues that competence alone is not enough; trust, resilience, sacrifice, and character are what turn a team into a true crew.
leadership.whyItMatters: Strong ministry teams are built on character as much as gifting, making this a useful lens for staff culture and volunteer leadership.

goodNews.title: King Charles III’s charity marks 50 years of helping young people find work - WHEC.com
goodNews.summary: The King’s Trust celebrated five decades of helping young people gain confidence, training, and employment, with stories of lives redirected through timely support.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is an encouraging reminder that patient investment in young people can bear lasting fruit—something churches are called to do well.
